articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Has a 16.5 Million Square-Foot Development Pipeline
articles.summary: JLL's new analysis reveals a massive 16.5 million square-foot pipeline of location-based entertainment development underway, signaling continued strong investor and developer confidence in the sector. This growth is largely driven by FECs and experiential entertainment filling vacated retail space. FEC operators and investors should take note of the competitive landscape expansion heading their way.

articles.title: E8 Properties Is Converting Vacant Retail Spaces Into Family Entertainment Centers
articles.summary: E8 Properties is actively transforming underperforming retail real estate into family entertainment centers, reflecting the broader trend of experiential tenants backfilling vacated anchor stores. This adaptive reuse strategy is gaining momentum with developers and landlords eager to drive foot traffic. FEC operators looking to expand should watch this model as a potential pathway to securing favorable lease terms in high-visibility retail corridors.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market: Key Trends and Investment Outlook
articles.summary: A new market report highlights investment trends and growth drivers shaping the location-based entertainment sector, covering areas directly relevant to FEC operators and suppliers. The report points to rising consumer demand for out-of-home experiences as a primary catalyst. Investors and operators can use this data to benchmark growth strategies and evaluate capital deployment opportunities.

articles.title: $3 Billion Motown Sports Village Planned Near Detroit Metro Airport
articles.summary: A massive $3 billion mixed-use entertainment complex is planned near Detroit Metro Airport, incorporating an arena, water park, hotels, IMAX theater, and more. Projects of this scale signal continued appetite from developers and capital markets for large-format entertainment destinations. FEC operators and suppliers should monitor this project as a potential partnership, supplier, or competitive benchmarking opportunity.

articles.title: Vintia and Momants AI Partner to Bring Conversational AI Ticketing to Attractions
articles.summary: Vintia has announced a partnership with Momants AI to integrate AI-powered conversational ticketing into its Enviso platform, enabling guests to purchase and manage tickets via WhatsApp, Instagram, email, chatbot, and in-app chat. The technology removes friction from the booking journey by replacing traditional checkout flows with natural, guided conversations. FEC operators using Enviso or evaluating ticketing upgrades should consider how conversational AI could boost conversion rates and reduce support overhead.

articles.title: FEC of the Year Hosts AAMA's FEC Connect Industry Event
articles.summary: RePlay Magazine reports that the AAMA's FEC Connect gathering was hosted by the FEC of the Year winner, bringing together operators and suppliers for industry networking and best-practice sharing. Events like FEC Connect are key touchpoints for staying current on operational trends and supplier innovations. FEC operators should look for recaps and takeaways from the event to benchmark their own venues.

articles.title: Expert Guide to Designing Accessible Attractions for Diverse Guests
articles.summary: A detailed expert guide outlines best practices for building accessibility into attraction design from the concept stage, emphasizing that 1.2 billion people globally have additional needs, making it one of the largest untapped market opportunities. Key recommendations include designing for dignity with shared boarding, embedding accessibility budgets into RFPs, and consulting guests with lived experience early in the process. FEC operators planning new builds or renovations should treat this guide as a practical checklist to avoid costly retrofits and expand their addressable audience.

articles.title: India's Indoor Entertainment Market Shows Strong Growth Trends
articles.summary: A new report examines the expanding indoor entertainment market in India, highlighting rising middle-class disposable income and urbanization as key demand drivers. The analysis covers growth in trampoline parks, arcades, bowling, and multi-attraction FEC formats. Global FEC suppliers and investors considering emerging market expansion will find this a useful strategic reference.

articles.title: Supercharged Entertainment Eyed for Polaris District Alongside Topgolf and Smash Park
articles.summary: A Supercharged entertainment venue is being considered for the Polaris entertainment corridor in Columbus, Ohio, which already features Topgolf and Smash Park, creating a dense cluster of competitive social entertainment options. This trend of entertainment districts concentrating multiple FEC-style operators in one area is reshaping how consumers choose destinations. FEC operators in similar markets should study co-location dynamics and differentiation strategies to remain competitive.

articles.title: Cinemark's Gamescape Entertainment Venue Scrapped in Merriam, Kansas
articles.summary: Cinemark's planned Gamescape location-based entertainment concept has been cancelled at the Merriam Town Center development, representing a setback for the cinema chain's FEC diversification strategy. The cancellation may reflect the challenges large cinema operators face when pivoting into the competitive entertainment center space. FEC operators can take note as a reminder that even well-capitalized entrants face significant hurdles in execution.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center Opens in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: A new Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center has opened its doors in Carrollton, Georgia, adding another multi-attraction FEC to the market. New venue openings in smaller markets indicate continued operator confidence in secondary cities as viable FEC destinations. Industry observers can watch Thunderzone's Carrollton location as a data point on suburban market viability.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Coming to Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A family entertainment center is planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, a fast-growing Nashville suburb, signaling continued FEC expansion into high-growth residential communities. The development reflects the broader industry trend of FECs targeting suburban population growth corridors. Operators and developers scouting new locations should note Mt. Juliet as an example of the demographic-driven site selection strategy.

articles.title: Rising Heat and Pollution Are Fueling the Boom in Indoor Play and Entertainment
articles.summary: An analysis from India highlights how increasing heat and urban pollution are accelerating demand for indoor play and entertainment facilities as families seek climate-controlled alternatives to outdoor activities. This macro trend has direct implications for FEC operators, particularly those with indoor-focused formats like arcades, trampoline parks, and laser tag. Operators can leverage weather-driven demand patterns in marketing and capacity planning strategies.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Expanding to Milford This Summer
articles.summary: Slick City, the indoor slide and action park concept, is opening a new location in Milford, continuing the brand's national rollout of its distinctive indoor attraction format. The chain's expansion is a relevant benchmark for FEC operators watching which attraction formats are gaining traction with investors and franchisees. Suppliers serving multi-location indoor attraction brands should take note of Slick City's growth trajectory.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool Ahead of June Launch
articles.summary: Tenpin is opening a new entertainment centre on Rigby Road in Blackpool, now taking bookings ahead of its June 5 opening date, adding bowling and associated entertainment to the competitive Blackpool leisure market. The opening reflects Tenpin's continued UK expansion strategy targeting leisure-destination towns. FEC operators in the bowling and multi-entertainment sector can use Tenpin's rollout as a competitive intelligence reference.

articles.title: Mattel-Branded Indoor Waterpark Planned for Weldon Mills Resort
articles.summary: A Mattel Wonder-branded indoor waterpark is planned as part of the new Weldon Mills Resort development, reflecting continued appetite for IP-licensed entertainment experiences in the resort and FEC space. The Mattel brand partnership signals how toy and entertainment IPs are increasingly being deployed in physical attractions to drive differentiation and family appeal. FEC operators considering IP licensing or themed attraction investments should watch this project closely.

articles.title: London-Born Indoor Entertainment Concept with Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-originated indoor entertainment venue blending foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, showcasing the European appetite for social entertainment formats that combine F&B with active gaming. This hybrid model mirrors trends seen in North American social FEC venues and may foreshadow further European expansion. Operators and suppliers tracking the global spread of social entertainment concepts will find this development relevant.

articles.title: Morey's Piers Kicks Off 2026 Season with New Attractions and Major Renovations
articles.summary: Morey's Piers in Wildwood, New Jersey is launching its 2026 season with new experiences, ride renovations, and the return of its refurbished Giant Wheel, demonstrating the importance of annual reinvestment to maintain guest interest. The park's approach of combining capital improvements with seasonal programming is a model relevant to FEC operators of all sizes. Operators planning their own reinvestment cycles can draw inspiration from Morey's multi-pronged refresh strategy.

articles.title: UK Government Cuts VAT to 5% for Theme Parks and Attractions This Summer
articles.summary: The UK Chancellor has announced a temporary VAT reduction from 20% to 5% on eligible attractions including theme parks, museums, and zoos from June 25 to September 1, as part of a 'Great British Summer Savings' scheme. The estimated £300 million initiative is designed to boost consumer spending on leisure and support attendance-dependent businesses during the summer season. UK-based FEC and attraction operators should prepare marketing campaigns to capitalize on the lower effective ticket prices and anticipated demand uplift.

articles.title: $60 Million Sports Complex Pitched as Economic Development Driver in Louisiana Parish
articles.summary: Parish leaders in Louisiana are advocating for a $60 million sports and entertainment complex, positioning it as an economic development engine for the region. Large-format sports and entertainment complexes increasingly compete with and complement traditional FECs in their markets. FEC operators, developers, and investors evaluating site selection in similar markets should factor in publicly funded competition and potential co-location opportunities.

articles.title: Savannah Skating Community Fights to Save Rink Following Closure
articles.summary: The closure of a skating rink in Savannah has sparked community pushback, with skaters organizing to preserve the facility as a cultural and social hub. Skating rink closures continue to affect communities across the US, often creating opportunities for new operators or conversion into other FEC formats. Operators considering skating-related investments should note the strong community demand signal these closures frequently generate.

articles.title: Massive Texas Indoor Amusement Center Draws Families with Diverse Attraction Mix
articles.summary: A feature on a large Texas indoor amusement center highlights the appeal of high-density, multi-attraction facilities offering nonstop entertainment for all age groups. The venue's format, combining multiple attraction types under one roof, represents a model many FEC operators are adopting to maximize dwell time and per-visit spend. Operators evaluating attraction mix and square footage optimization can use this as a real-world reference point.

articles.title: DreamLab Immersive Positions AI-First Design Studio for Attractions and LBE Markets
articles.summary: DreamLab Immersive, based in Orlando, is positioning itself as a leader in AI-assisted design and production for themed entertainment, attractions, location-based experiences, and hospitality. The studio's AI-first approach to immersive environment creation could offer FEC operators faster and more cost-effective pathways to high-quality themed experiences. Operators planning new attractions or refreshes should be aware of emerging design studios leveraging generative AI to reduce production timelines and costs.

articles.title: America 250 Wheel to Make Midwest Debut at Wisconsin State Fair
articles.summary: The America 250 Wheel, the largest wheel ever to appear at Wisconsin State Fair, will make its Midwest debut at the fair's 175th anniversary celebration, offering a major draw for the event. Large-scale portable attractions like this demonstrate the revenue potential of marquee rides as event anchors. FEC operators and event producers can take note of how destination-worthy attractions drive attendance and media coverage.

articles.title: Dorney Park Donates 5,000 Squishmallows in Community Partnership Initiative
articles.summary: Dorney Park partnered with local charitable organizations to donate approximately 5,000 Squishmallows to children in the Lehigh Valley region, showcasing how amusement and FEC venues can build community goodwill through prize inventory donations. Community partnerships of this kind can enhance brand perception and strengthen local relationships for FEC operators. This is a low-cost, high-visibility CSR model that smaller FEC operators can readily adapt.

articles.title: Theme Parks Should Be Transparent About the Real Causes of Attendance Declines
articles.summary: A commentary piece argues that major theme parks should be more candid with investors and the public about the true drivers behind flagging attendance, rather than attributing dips solely to external factors. Honest communication about value perception, pricing, and experience quality can build longer-term consumer trust. FEC operators can draw lessons from this analysis when crafting their own messaging around attendance trends and guest satisfaction.

articles.title: Rising Temperatures Linked to Growing Demand for Indoor Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: An expert analysis from Nigeria draws a direct connection between rising ambient temperatures and increased consumer interest in indoor entertainment options, a trend being observed across multiple global markets. Climate-driven behavioral shifts toward indoor leisure present a structural tailwind for FEC operators with climate-controlled facilities. Operators can incorporate weather and climate data into demand forecasting and marketing timing strategies.

articles.title: Hersheypark and Union in Contract Negotiations as Season Opening Approaches
articles.summary: Hersheypark is engaged in union contract negotiations as the park prepares to open for its summer season, highlighting the labor relations challenges facing large entertainment venues. Staffing and labor costs remain among the top operational concerns for FEC and amusement park operators heading into peak season. FEC operators managing seasonal workforces should monitor the outcome of this negotiation as a potential bellwether for labor cost pressures across the industry.
